claim: Claude Skills are SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures) for agents, packaging composable procedural knowledge in folders with instructions, scripts, and resources
actionable: Think of skills as SOPs for autonomous agents: create a skill folder with skill.md (instructions), helper scripts, and reference files that Claude loads when relevant

claim: Skills are composable: multiple skills work together with Claude automatically coordinating which to use
actionable: Design individual skills to be reusable and focused; Claude will orchestrate them together without explicit routing

claim: Skills are portable across Claude Chat, Claude Code, and the API, making them platform-agnostic
actionable: Build a skill once and deploy it in Claude Chat, Claude Code, and via the Anthropic API without modification

claim: Use code inside skills instead of just plain English because code outputs more reliably than verbose instructions alone
actionable: When building a skill, include Python or JavaScript code snippets in the skill.md to make instructions more concrete and less ambiguous

claim: Anthropic released Apache 2 licensed skills in their GitHub repo plus some proprietary skills to establish standards and protect from competitors copying
actionable: Review Anthropic's skills GitHub repo to see best practices; if you want to protect your custom skills from being reused by competitors, consider licensing them restrictively

claim: Turn off training on your skills in Claude settings if you don't want Anthropic using them to improve future models
actionable: Go to Claude settings and disable 'Allow Anthropic to improve Claude with this data' for sensitive skills you create

claim: Skills markdown files (skill.md) are the core instruction layer similar to cursor rules or agents.md files used in coding
actionable: Create a skill.md in each skill folder that describes steps, syntax, and tool usage, similar to how you'd write agent instructions in other tools
